notes: Positives test whether the un-hardcoded skill surfaces the canonical book for each domain (the generalization test), gives an apt distillation, and actually applies the framework downstream. Negatives are near-misses that should NOT trigger the full wisdom-first flow.

evals.prompt: 下周我要在公司全员大会上做一个 20 分钟的演讲，介绍我们团队明年的方向。我特别紧张，不知道怎么开场、怎么让大家记住我讲的东西。帮我想想这个演讲怎么准备。
evals.expected_output: Recommends As We Speak (Peter Meyers & Shann Nix Jones) or a clearly canonical public-speaking work; distills its core ideas (content/delivery/state, storytelling, presence); then builds an actual speech plan using those ideas.
evals.target_canon: As We Speak (Peter Meyers & Shann Nix Jones)
expectations: Recommends As We Speak, or names it among 1-2 canonical public-speaking books
Gives a concise, accurate distillation of the framework's core moves
The speech plan visibly applies the framework (opening, memorability) rather than generic tips
Recommends the user read the book to go deeper

evals.prompt: I run a mid-size B2B SaaS company. We're spread too thin across five products and growth has stalled. I need to figure out our strategy for next year. Where do I even start?
evals.expected_output: Recommends Playing to Win (A.G. Lafley & Roger Martin); distills the five strategic choices cascade (winning aspiration, where to play, how to win, capabilities, management systems); then applies the cascade to the user's stalled-growth situation.
evals.target_canon: Playing to Win (A.G. Lafley & Roger Martin)
expectations: Recommends Playing to Win (Lafley & Martin) or names it as the canonical strategy book here
Distills the strategy cascade / where-to-play-how-to-win accurately
Applies the cascade concretely to the five-product, stalled-growth problem
Recommends reading the book

evals.prompt: 我最近特别迷茫。工作干了五年感觉每天都在原地打转，生活也乱糟糟的，时间永远不够用，对什么都提不起劲。不知道该怎么调整。
evals.expected_output: Leads with warmth; recommends The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People (Stephen Covey); distills relevant habits (be proactive, begin with the end in mind, put first things first, circle of influence); then helps the user apply them to their specific rut.
evals.target_canon: The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People (Stephen Covey)
expectations: Responds with empathy before jumping to frameworks
Recommends The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People (Covey)
Distills relevant habits accurately (e.g. proactivity, end in mind, first things first)
Applies the habits to the user's actual feeling-stuck situation

evals.prompt: 我和我老婆最近经常因为一些小事吵架，比如家务分工、孩子教育。每次都从小事升级成互相指责，谁也不让谁。我想改善我们的沟通，但不知道怎么开口。
evals.expected_output: Leads with empathy; recommends Nonviolent Communication (Marshall Rosenberg); distills the four components (observations, feelings, needs, requests); then scripts how the user might actually open a conversation with their spouse using NVC.
expectations: Responds with empathy and does not take sides clinically
Recommends Nonviolent Communication (Marshall Rosenberg)
Distills the four components: observation, feeling, need, request
Provides concrete NVC-structured phrasing for the user's actual situation

evals.prompt: 我要给老板写一封邮件，汇报我们项目延期的情况和接下来的补救方案。内容有点多有点乱，我一写就啰嗦，老板看了肯定抓不住重点。怎么写才清楚？
evals.expected_output: Recommends The Pyramid Principle (Barbara Minto) and the MECE principle; distills top-down/answer-first structure and MECE grouping; then restructures the user's delay-report email using the pyramid.
expectations: Recommends The Pyramid Principle (Barbara Minto)
Explains MECE (mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive)
Distills answer-first / top-down structuring accurately
Restructures the user's actual email using the pyramid structure

evals.domain: negotiation (generalization test, not in skill body)
evals.prompt: 我拿到一个新 offer，想用它去找现在的老板谈加薪。但我怕谈崩了反而尴尬，也不知道开价多少合适。帮我想想这次加薪谈话怎么谈。
evals.expected_output: Surfaces principled negotiation — ideally Getting to Yes (Fisher & Ury) and/or Never Split the Difference (Voss); distills BATNA / interests-vs-positions; then plans the raise conversation around the user's BATNA (the competing offer).
evals.target_canon: Getting to Yes (Fisher & Ury) and/or Never Split the Difference (Chris Voss)
expectations: Recommends a canonical negotiation book (Getting to Yes and/or Never Split the Difference)
Introduces BATNA and interests-vs-positions
Plans the raise conversation explicitly using the competing offer as BATNA

evals.domain: habit / personal productivity (generalization test, not in skill body)
evals.prompt: 我一直想养成早起跑步的习惯，但每次坚持不了一周就放弃了。试过很多次都失败。有什么办法能真的坚持下来吗？
evals.expected_output: Surfaces Atomic Habits (James Clear) or a canonical habit-formation work; distills the four laws / systems-over-goals / friction reduction; then designs a concrete habit system for early-morning running.
expectations: Recommends Atomic Habits (James Clear) or a clearly canonical habits book
Distills the four laws or systems-over-goals accurately
Designs a concrete, friction-reducing plan for the running habit

evals.prompt: A.G. Lafley 一共写过哪几本书？
evals.expected_output: A direct factual answer. Should NOT launch the full wisdom-first framework-and-book flow — this is a lookup about an author, not a consulting task.
expectations: Answers the factual question directly
Does NOT run the full frame-distill-recommend-apply sequence

evals.prompt: 帮我把这段 CSV 转成 JSON 格式，第一行是表头。
evals.expected_output: Just does the conversion. No framework, no book recommendation.
expectations: Performs the conversion directly
Does NOT recommend any framework or book
